documents.title: Client Opportunity Summary – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum

sections.heading: Client Overview
sections.content:
Brand: Lumière — a clean beauty indie brand.
Product: Vitamin C Brightening Serum, developed from scratch with Drunk Elephant C-Firma as an aesthetic benchmark.
Target Consumer: Millennial women, clean beauty enthusiasts.

sections.heading: Commercial Parameters
sections.content:
Retail Price Point: $65–$85 USD.
Initial MOQ: 3,000 units, scaling to approximately 10,000 units within 12 months.
Target Launch: Q4 of this year (~8 months from now).

sections.heading: Claims & Positioning
sections.content:
Approved claims: brightening, even skin tone, antioxidant protection.
No drug claims permitted. All claims must remain cosmetic in nature per FDA guidelines.

sections.heading: Markets
sections.content:
Primary: United States (Q4 launch).
Secondary: Canada (within 6 months post-US launch).

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

documents.title: Sales Proposal – Lumière Vitamin C Brightening Serum

sections.heading: Proposed Scope of Work
sections.content:
• Full custom formulation development (vitamin C brightening serum)
• Stability and compatibility testing
• Claims support documentation (brightening, antioxidant, even skin tone)
• US and Canada regulatory compliance review
• Labeling review for both markets

sections.heading: Recommended Formula Direction
sections.content:
Develop a vitamin C serum (L-ascorbic acid or stable derivative) benchmarked aesthetically against Drunk Elephant C-Firma, meeting clean beauty ingredient standards.
Formula should support brightening and antioxidant claims without crossing into drug-claim territory.

sections.heading: Pricing & Volume Structure
sections.content:
Initial run: 3,000 units — pricing to be quoted at this tier.
Scale pricing available at 10,000 units within 12 months; recommend tiered pricing agreement to secure the account.
Final unit pricing: [To be confirmed by internal costing team].

sections.heading: Proposed Timeline
sections.content:
Target Q4 launch requires formulation kickoff within the next 2–3 weeks.
Key milestones: formula brief (Week 1–2), prototype (Week 6–8), stability initiation (Week 10), final approval and production (Month 6–7).

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

documents.title: Project Timeline & Milestone Plan – Lumière Vitamin C Serum

sections.heading: Phase 1 – Formulation Development (Weeks 1–8)
sections.content:
• Week 1–2: Formula brief sign-off and clean beauty ingredient screening
• Week 4–6: Initial prototype development
• Week 7–8: Internal evaluation and client prototype submission

sections.heading: Phase 2 – Testing & Compliance (Weeks 9–18)
sections.content:
• Week 9–10: Stability and compatibility testing initiation
• Week 12–14: Claims substantiation review (brightening, antioxidant)
• Week 15–18: US and Canada regulatory/labeling compliance review

sections.heading: Phase 3 – Production & Launch (Months 6–8)
sections.content:
• Month 6–7: Formula lock, artwork approval, and bulk production for 3,000-unit run
• Month 8: Finished goods delivery, Q4 US launch target met
• Month 14: Canada launch (6 months post-US), 10,000-unit scale production

sections.heading: Key Risks & Mitigations
sections.content:
• Risk: Vitamin C stability issues — Mitigation: evaluate multiple vitamin C forms early in development
• Risk: Timeline compression — Mitigation: parallel-path stability and regulatory review where possible

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

documents.title: Regulatory & Market Compliance Overview – US & Canada

sections.heading: United States (FDA – Cosmetic)
sections.content:
Product classified as a cosmetic under FDA regulations; claims (brightening, antioxidant, even skin tone) are permissible cosmetic claims.
Must comply with MoCRA (Modernization of Cosmetics Regulation Act) requirements including facility registration and product listing.
Labeling must meet FDA 21 CFR Part 701 requirements.

sections.heading: Canada (Health Canada – Cosmetic)
sections.content:
Product must be notified to Health Canada within 10 days of first sale under the Cosmetic Regulations (Canada Consumer Product Safety Act).
Ingredient list must use INCI names; bilingual labeling (English and French) required.
Clean beauty ingredient standards should be cross-referenced against Health Canada's Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist.

sections.heading: Claims Compliance
sections.content:
Permitted claims: brightening, even skin tone, antioxidant protection — all cosmetic in nature.
No drug claims (e.g., "treats hyperpigmentation" or "clinically corrects") may be used in either market.
Claims substantiation documentation is recommended for retail and e-commerce listings.

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

sections.heading: Account Summary
sections.content:
Client: Lumière (clean beauty indie brand).
Contact: [To be confirmed].
Opportunity: Custom vitamin C brightening serum, full development scope.

sections.heading: Key Selling Points for This Account
sections.content:
• Full-service formulation capability — client needs development from scratch
• Clean beauty expertise aligns with brand values
• Ability to support dual-market launch (US + Canada) from one manufacturing partner
• Scalable volume pricing from 3,000 to 10,000+ units

sections.heading: Competitive Context
sections.content:
Client has referenced Drunk Elephant C-Firma as an aesthetic benchmark — position our capability to match premium texture and efficacy at an indie-brand-accessible MOQ.
Emphasize clean ingredient compliance and claims expertise as differentiators.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.content:
• Send formal proposal and timeline to client within 5 business days
• Schedule formulation kickoff call with R&D team
• Confirm unit pricing with costing team at 3,000 and 10,000-unit tiers
